name: Kotlin/JS
description: Kotlin transpiled to JavaScript for type-safe, multiplatform web development with JetBrains tooling

description.value: Kotlin transpiled to JavaScript for type-safe, multiplatform web development with JetBrains tooling

initial-release.comment: Kotlin 1.1 (March 2017) introduced JavaScript interoperability; Kotlin 1.0 was released February 2016 but JS support came later

github-stars.comment: As of December 2025; this is for Kotlin language overall, not JS-specific

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained by JetBrains with regular releases; 2.3.0 released Dec 2025 with JS-specific improvements

reactivity-model.comment: Depends on framework: KVision uses Snabbdom virtual DOM; Kobweb/Kilua use Compose-based fine-grained reactivity

component-model.comment: Supports both: Kotlin wrappers for React use functional components; KVision uses object-oriented class-based components

rendering-paradigm.comment: All major Kotlin/JS frameworks use declarative rendering: type-safe DSLs, React wrappers, or Compose HTML

state-management-approach.comment: Frameworks like KVision and fritz2 have built-in state management; can also use React ecosystem patterns

opinionatedness.comment: Kotlin/JS itself is flexible; you can choose between KVision (opinionated), Kobweb (Next.js-like), or React wrappers

backward-compatibility.comment: JetBrains committed to long-term backward compatibility since Kotlin 1.0; Kotlin/JS IR backend stable since 1.8

primary-language.comment: Kotlin is the primary language; compiles to JavaScript

compiles-to.comment: Kotlin/JS compiles to JavaScript (ES5 or ES2015); Kotlin/Wasm is a separate target for WebAssembly

typescript-support.comment: Kotlin has its own type system; can generate .d.ts TypeScript definitions for interop, but TypeScript not used directly

template-syntax.comment: Type-safe DSLs for HTML: kotlinx.html, Compose HTML, or React wrapper DSL; no templates or JSX

styling-approach.comment: Kobweb provides type-safe CSS-in-Kotlin; styled-components available via wrappers; external CSS also works

build-required.comment: Kotlin compiler required; uses Gradle with Kotlin Multiplatform plugin

wasm-interop.comment: Kotlin/JS compiles to JavaScript, not WebAssembly; Kotlin/Wasm is a separate compilation target

reactive-primitives.comment: fritz2 uses Kotlin Flows; KVision has observable state; Kobweb/Kilua use Compose State

computed-values.comment: Via Kotlin Flows, derivedStateOf in Compose, or computed properties in KVision

two-way-binding.comment: KVision supports two-way data binding for forms; Kobweb uses controlled components

event-handling.comment: Declarative event handling via DSL: onClick, onInput, etc. in all Kotlin/JS frameworks

conditional-rendering.comment: Via Kotlin if/when expressions in DSL; type-safe conditional rendering

list-rendering.comment: Via Kotlin collections and forEach/map functions in DSL

component-slots.comment: Via lambda parameters (children content) in DSL; Compose-based frameworks use content slots

teleport-portal.comment: Available in React wrappers via createPortal; Kobweb has PopupPlacement

suspense.comment: React Suspense available via kotlin-react wrappers

error-boundaries.comment: React Error Boundaries available via kotlin-react wrappers

context-provide-inject.comment: React Context via wrappers; Compose-based frameworks have CompositionLocal

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Direct DOM access via kotlinx.browser.document; refs available in React wrappers

official-router.comment: Kobweb has built-in file-based routing; React Router available via wrappers

router-name.comment: Kobweb provides built-in Next.js-style routing; React Router available via kotlin-react-router

nested-routes.comment: Kobweb supports nested routes via directory structure; React Router wrapper supports nested routes

dynamic-routes.comment: Kobweb supports dynamic routes with parameters via file naming conventions

route-guards.comment: Can implement route guards in Kotlin code; React Router hooks available

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Webpack code splitting available; Kobweb supports deferred loading

scroll-restoration.comment: Available via browser APIs or React Router wrapper

built-in-state.comment: All Kotlin/JS frameworks provide local component state mechanisms

built-in-global-state.comment: fritz2 has built-in stores; KVision has StateFlow integration; Kobweb has built-in state

global-state-library.comment: fritz2 has built-in stores; kotlin-react-redux provides Redux integration

devtools-state-inspection.comment: React DevTools work with kotlin-react; Redux DevTools via kotlin-react-redux

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via localStorage APIs or community libraries

time-travel-debugging.comment: Via Redux DevTools when using kotlin-react-redux

official-cli.comment: Kobweb has its own CLI; projects typically use Gradle with Kotlin Multiplatform plugin

hot-module-replacement.comment: Webpack HMR supported; Kobweb provides live-reloading; KVision supports HMR

browser-devtools.comment: React DevTools work with kotlin-react; standard browser devtools for debugging

vs-code-extension.comment: Kotlin extension available but IntelliJ IDEA provides the best Kotlin experience

error-messages.comment: Kotlin compiler provides helpful error messages with type information; IDE integration excellent in IntelliJ

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive Kotlin/JS docs from JetBrains; individual frameworks (Kobweb, KVision) have good documentation

interactive-tutorial.comment: Kotlin Koans provide interactive learning; supports JS target

playground.comment: Official Kotlin Playground supports JS and JS IR targets

bundle-size-core.comment: Bundle size varies significantly based on framework and DCE; Kotlin stdlib adds overhead. Reports of 1MB+ for simple apps before DCE

bundle-size-full.comment: Full bundle sizes vary widely (reported 30MB+ before optimization in some cases); heavily dependent on DCE effectiveness

virtual-dom.comment: KVision uses Snabbdom virtual DOM; React wrappers use React's virtual DOM

compile-time-optimization.comment: Kotlin compiler performs optimizations; IR backend enables better dead code elimination

tree-shaking.comment: Webpack tree shaking + Kotlin DCE (dead code elimination); IR backend improves tree shaking

code-splitting.comment: Webpack code splitting available in Kotlin/JS projects

lazy-components.comment: React.lazy available via kotlin-react; dynamic imports supported

ssr-capable.comment: Kilua supports true SSR; Kobweb supports static site export; Node.js server rendering possible

streaming-ssr.comment: Limited streaming SSR support in Kotlin/JS frameworks

partial-hydration.comment: No islands architecture in current Kotlin/JS frameworks

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Kotlin/JS not included in js-framework-benchmark main results

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Kotlin/JS distributed via Gradle/Maven, not npm; kotlin npm package exists but not primary distribution

component-libraries.comment: KVision components, Kobweb Silk, React wrappers (Material UI, Bootstrap, etc.), Kilua components

meta-framework.comment: Kobweb is Next.js-inspired with file-based routing, SSG export, and built-in features

major-companies-using.comment: Kotlin/JS adoption is typically within larger Kotlin Multiplatform strategies; JetBrains uses internally

official-sponsor-backer.comment: JetBrains created and maintains Kotlin; provides official kotlin-wrappers for JS ecosystem

discord-community-size.comment: Kotlin has Slack (kotlinlang.slack.com) and Discord communities; JS-specific channels within larger Kotlin community

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Approximate count for kotlin-js tag; Kotlin overall has 80,000+ questions

job-market-demand.comment: Niche for web; Kotlin job demand is primarily for Android/backend. Web-specific Kotlin/JS jobs are rare

official-testing-library.comment: kotlin-test supports JS target; integrates with Jasmine, Mocha, Jest, QUnit

testing-library-name.comment: kotlin-test is official; Kotest provides additional testing capabilities for Kotlin/JS

component-testing.comment: Component testing via kotlin-test with Karma/Mocha runners

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Playwright, Cypress work with Kotlin/JS output; standard browser testing

snapshot-testing.comment: Available via Jest integration or custom test runners

mobile-framework.comment: Share code with native mobile via Kotlin Multiplatform; Compose Multiplatform for UI

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; can be added manually via service workers and manifest

desktop-framework.comment: Compose Multiplatform supports desktop; Kotlin/JS can run in Electron

learning-curve.comment: Requires learning Kotlin language, Gradle build system, and chosen framework (KVision, Kobweb, etc.)

concepts-to-learn.comment: Kotlin basics, null safety, coroutines, Gradle, Kotlin Multiplatform, JS interop, chosen framework patterns

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~15 minutes with Kobweb CLI; longer with manual Gradle setup; requires JDK and Gradle

migration-path.comment: No migration tools from JavaScript frameworks; requires rewrite in Kotlin

incremental-adoption.comment: Kotlin/JS can be embedded in existing pages; can call JS from Kotlin and vice versa
